page.title: Create Asset Template
page.description: Create a new asset template with data processing scripts

scriptConvention.title: Result variable
scriptConvention.textPrefix: every script must end by assigning
scriptConvention.textSuffix: with the value the next step should receive. Do NOT use `return X` at the top level — it is silently ignored. Throw with `throw new Error(...)` to fail the pipeline.

stepper.title: Configure Asset Template
stepper.subtitle: Complete all steps to create a new template
stepper.requiredInfo: All fields marked with * are required

step1.label: Basic Information
step1.description: Add template name, manufacturer, and device details
step1.title: Basic Information
step1.subtitle: Add template name, manufacturer, and device details

name.label: Name
name.hint: A descriptive name for this asset template

description.label: Description
description.placeholder: Enter a description for this template
description.hint: Optional description of what this template is for

isTemplate.label: Shared Template
isTemplate.hint: Mark as shared template for use by child organizations

step2.label: Asset ID Path
step2.description: Define the path to identify your assets
step2.title: Asset ID Path
step2.subtitle: Define the path to identify your assets

banner.title: What is Asset ID Path?
banner.description: The Asset ID Path is a dot-notation path that points to the unique identifier in your incoming payload. This identifier is used to uniquely identify and track your assets.

assetIdPath.label: Asset ID Path
assetIdPath.hint: Use dot notation to specify the path to your asset's unique identifier

step3.label: Preprocessor Script
step3.title: Preprocessor Script
step3.subtitle: Prepare and normalize incoming data before validation. This step is optional but useful for data transformation.

exampleCode.comment2: Prepare and normalize incoming data before validation

step4.label: Validation Script
step4.title: Validation Script
step4.subtitle: Validate data integrity and format. Return true if valid, false or throw an Error if invalid.

guidelines.title: Script Guidelines
guidelines.expectedReturnValue: const result = <validated payload>; — throw Error to fail

exampleCode.comment4: Return: true if valid, false or throw Error if invalid
exampleCode.rangeError: Temperature out of valid range (-50 to 100)

step5.label: Conversion Script
step5.title: Conversion Script
step5.subtitle: Convert validated data to MapexOS standard format. This ensures consistency across the platform.

banner.important: Important: Your script must return a StandardizedPayload. Check the Script Guidelines below for the required format.

guidelines.title: Script Guidelines
guidelines.standardizedPayloadDescription: Your conversion script MUST return an object with the following structure:

exampleCode.comment2: Convert data to MapexOS standard format

step6.label: Test Payload
step6.title: Test Payload
step6.subtitle: Provide sample data for testing your scripts

banner.info: This payload will be used to test all your scripts (preprocessor, validation, conversion, and test script).

guidelines.title: Test Guidelines
guidelines.expectedReturn: Expected return: Boolean (true if test passes) or throw Error

exampleCode.comment2: Additional test logic for your payload
exampleCode.comment4: Return: true if test passes, false or throw Error if fails

step7.label: Testing & Review
step7.description: Test your scripts and verify all details
step7.title: Testing
step7.subtitle: Test your scripts with the provided payload

banner.testRequired: Tests must pass successfully before proceeding to Review. Run the tests below to validate your configuration.
banner.standardizedPayloadInfo: Your conversion script must return a StandardizedPayload.

testSection.title: Test Your Scripts
testSection.modifyPayloadHint: To modify the test payload, go back to Step 6

testResults.label: Test Results
testResults.emptyState: Click "Run Test" to test your scripts

reviewSection.title: Review Configuration
reviewSection.description: Review all configuration before saving your Asset Template.

step8.label: Dynamic Fields
step8.description: Configure custom fields for event queries
step8.title: Dynamic Fields Mapping
step8.subtitle: Configure custom fields that can be used to search and filter events from this asset template.

banner.title: Custom Searchable Fields
banner.description: Fields added here will be indexed and available for filtering and searching events. Map payload fields to custom names and types for easy querying.

noFieldsWarning.title: No Available Fields
noFieldsWarning.description: Run tests in Step 7 first to extract available fields from your payload.

emptyState.title: No fields mapped yet
emptyState.description: Add field mappings above to enable custom filtering and searching for events.

step9.label: Review
step9.title: Review Configuration
step9.subtitle: Review all configuration before saving your Asset Template.
step9.noFieldsWarning: No fields detected. Run tests in Step 7 to extract fields.

pageOverview.title: Asset Template Wizard
pageOverview.description: This wizard guides you through creating an Asset Template. An Asset Template defines how MapexOS processes data from your devices (IoT, ERP, Webhooks, APIs, etc.).

stepper.title: Navigation Steps
stepper.description: Use the stepper to navigate between steps. Each step configures a specific part of your template. In edit mode, you can click any step to jump directly to it.

assetIdPath.title: Asset ID Path (Required)
assetIdPath.description: Every event payload must have a unique identifier (UUID). Here you specify the path to that field using dot notation (e.g., payload.deviceId). This ID uniquely identifies each asset in the system.

preprocessorScript.title: Preprocessor Script (Optional)
preprocessorScript.description: Use this step to transform or normalize the incoming payload before validation. Useful for adding timestamps, renaming fields, or restructuring data. Skip if your payload is already in the expected format.

validationScript.title: Validation Script (Optional)
validationScript.description: Validate that all required fields are present and data is in the correct format. MapexOS provides a validation library you can use. See documentation: https://mapexos.io/docs/1.0.0/en/core-platform/assets-and-templates.html#validation-helpers-mapex-validator

conversionScript.title: Conversion Script (Required)
conversionScript.description: This is REQUIRED. Your script must convert the payload to MapexOS StandardizedPayload format. This ensures all data follows a consistent structure across the platform.

testPayload.title: Test Payload (Required)
testPayload.description: Provide a sample payload that your asset will send. This payload is used to test all your scripts (preprocessor, validation, conversion) and ensure they work correctly.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: Run tests to verify all scripts follow ECMAScript standards and execute without errors. Tests must pass before you can proceed to the next step.

dynamicFields.title: Dynamic Fields (Optional)
dynamicFields.description: Since MapexOS is generic, you can select fields from your payload to use for searching and filtering events. These fields become queryable in the Event Store.

review.title: Review
review.description: Review all your configuration before saving. You can go back to any step to make changes if needed.

notifications.loadFailed: Failed to load asset template. Redirecting to list...
notifications.validationError: Please fill in all required fields.
notifications.conversionScriptRequired: Conversion Script is required. Please add a conversion script before proceeding.
notifications.testsMustPass: Tests must pass successfully before proceeding to Review. Please run and pass all tests.
notifications.validationFailed: Validation error. Please check the data and try again.
notifications.alreadyExists: An Asset Template with this name already exists.
notifications.networkError: Network error. Please check your connection and try again.
